name: Glaucoma
description: Glaucoma is elevated intraocular pressure that damages the optic nerve and retina, causing pain and vision loss. Primary glaucoma is hereditary; secondary glaucoma results from other eye conditions. It is a veterinary emergency in acute form.
treatment_overview: Emergency medical treatment aims to reduce intraocular pressure using topical and systemic medications. Long-term management requires ongoing medication. Affected eyes that are blind and painful are enucleated.
prevention_tips: Annual tonometry (pressure checks) in at-risk breeds
DNA test available for some breeds
Treat lens luxation and uveitis promptly to prevent secondary glaucoma
